title: Nerv Base names
wikitext:
[]: Nerv Base names (Spoilers)             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jun 28, 2009 12:20 pm Three Nerv bases that we know of from 2.0- Golgotha (Hakone), Tabgha (The moon base) and Bethany (Somewhere in the US or Russia). All three are named for places related to Jesus Christ. Golgotha, or Calvary, was where he was crucified...and no kidding, Hakone's where Lilith is crucified deep below. So...does anyone think the Nerv base names really link to the bibilical places and their significance, or is this another Anno troll job? Golgotha has me thinking that the names really do have meaning. Any ideas?

[]:             Quote Post by DanteNeverCry » Sun Jun 28, 2009 12:27 pm Cheap symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by Rommel » Sun Jun 28, 2009 12:31 pm Probably a troll job. Although it is more subtle that using more obvious biblical names like Nazareth or something.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Jun 28, 2009 12:31 pm Well, Lilth crucified pretty much explains Gologotha. Tabgha was the site of the third resurrection appearance of Jesus, and Kaworu was presumably born at Tabgha base. Hmm.... Mari comes from Bethany base, and there was a Mary of Bethany, a sister of Lazarus who became a follower of Jesus. Mari having some horrible family related tragedy in her past, and expects to solve it through piloting Eva (that would be *really* original)? Also, some connection between Mari and Kaworu?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 28, 2009 1:48 pm DanteNeverCry wrote: Cheap symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jun 28, 2009 1:50 pm Cheap pedantry. Just like the series OP.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Jun 28, 2009 2:10 pm NAveryW wrote: DanteNeverCry wrote: Cheap symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 28, 2009 2:14 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: NAveryW wrote: DanteNeverCry wrote: Cheap symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sun Jun 28, 2009 2:56 pm And many times the meaning of those symbols differs from the original one. They then get a new and exclusive "In-Eva meaning".

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jun 28, 2009 8:13 pm So the overwhelming consensus is that it's just Anno's pedantry again?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Jun 28, 2009 9:57 pm Until we know otherwise, Bethany and Tabgha are just bible-soundy. Golgotha is appropriately named, but only because they already have pointless crucifiction imagery.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Jun 29, 2009 1:29 am Anno: I picked the name "Evangelion" because it sounds complicated.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Jun 29, 2009 3:55 am Uriel Septim VII wrote: Anno: I picked the name "Evangelion" because it sounds complicated.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Jun 29, 2009 7:31 am I've always enjoyed the religious symbolism in Evangelion. Even if they are sometimes shallow and meaningless (666 firewall comes to mind), there's just something cool about Giant Cross explosions and complicated biblical names. Nothing says impending apocalypse quite like Biblical References.
